Name: Amazon Athena

Environments.Name: environment3
Environments.Description: The Amazon Athena endpoint for China (Beijing) and China (Ningxia)

Environments.Name: environment4
Environments.Description: The Amazon Athena endpoint for China (Beijing) and China (Ningxia)

Description:
Amazon Athena is an interactive query service that lets you use standard SQL to analyze data directly in Amazon S3. You can point Athena at your data in Amazon S3 and run ad-hoc queries and get results in seconds. Athena is serverless, so there is no infrastructure to set up or manage. You pay only for the queries you run. Athena scales automatically—executing queries in parallel—so results are fast, even with large datasets and complex queries. For more information, see What is Amazon Athena in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

If you connect to Athena using the JDBC driver, use version 1.1.0 of the driver or later with the Amazon Athena API. Earlier version drivers do not support the API. For more information and to download the driver, see Accessing Amazon Athena with JDBC.

For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

Endpoints.Name: BatchGetNamedQuery
Endpoints.Description: Returns the details of a single named query or a list of up to 50 queries, which you provide as an array of query ID strings. Requires you to have access to the workgroup in which the queries were saved. Use ListNamedQueriesInput to get the list of named query IDs in the specified workgroup. If information could not be retrieved for a submitted query ID, information about the query ID submitted is listed under UnprocessedNamedQueryId. Named queries differ from executed queries. Use BatchGetQueryExecutionInput to get details about each unique query execution, and ListQueryExecutionsInput to get a list of query execution IDs.

Endpoints.Name: BatchGetPreparedStatement
Endpoints.Description: Returns the details of a single prepared statement or a list of up to 256 prepared statements for the array of prepared statement names that you provide. Requires you to have access to the workgroup to which the prepared statements belong. If a prepared statement cannot be retrieved for the name specified, the statement is listed in UnprocessedPreparedStatementNames.

Endpoints.Name: BatchGetQueryExecution
Endpoints.Description: Returns the details of a single query execution or a list of up to 50 query executions, which you provide as an array of query execution ID strings. Requires you to have access to the workgroup in which the queries ran. To get a list of query execution IDs, use ListQueryExecutionsInput$WorkGroup. Query executions differ from named (saved) queries. Use BatchGetNamedQueryInput to get details about named queries.

Endpoints.Name: CreateDataCatalog
Endpoints.Description: Creates (registers) a data catalog with the specified name and properties. Catalogs created are visible to all users of the same Amazon Web Services account.

Endpoints.Name: CreateNamedQuery
Endpoints.Description:
Creates a named query in the specified workgroup. Requires that you have access to the workgroup.

For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

Endpoints.Name: CreateNotebook
Endpoints.Description: Creates an empty ipynb file in the specified Apache Spark enabled workgroup. Throws an error if a file in the workgroup with the same name already exists.

Endpoints.Name: CreatePreparedStatement
Endpoints.Description: Creates a prepared statement for use with SQL queries in Athena.

Endpoints.Name: CreatePresignedNotebookUrl
Endpoints.Description: Gets an authentication token and the URL at which the notebook can be accessed. During programmatic access, CreatePresignedNotebookUrl must be called every 10 minutes to refresh the authentication token. For information about granting programmatic access, see Grant programmatic access.

Endpoints.Name: CreateWorkGroup
Endpoints.Description: Creates a workgroup with the specified name. A workgroup can be an Apache Spark enabled workgroup or an Athena SQL workgroup.

Endpoints.Name: DeleteDataCatalog
Endpoints.Description: Deletes a data catalog.

Endpoints.Name: DeleteNamedQuery
Endpoints.Description:
Deletes the named query if you have access to the workgroup in which the query was saved.

For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

Endpoints.Name: DeleteNotebook
Endpoints.Description: Deletes the specified notebook.

Endpoints.Name: DeletePreparedStatement
Endpoints.Description: Deletes the prepared statement with the specified name from the specified workgroup.

Endpoints.Name: DeleteWorkGroup
Endpoints.Description: Deletes the workgroup with the specified name. The primary workgroup cannot be deleted.

Endpoints.Name: ExportNotebook
Endpoints.Description: Exports the specified notebook and its metadata.

Endpoints.Name: GetCalculationExecution
Endpoints.Description: Describes a previously submitted calculation execution.

Endpoints.Name: GetCalculationExecutionCode
Endpoints.Description: Retrieves the unencrypted code that was executed for the calculation.

Endpoints.Name: GetCalculationExecutionStatus
Endpoints.Description: Gets the status of a current calculation.

Endpoints.Name: GetDataCatalog
Endpoints.Description: Returns the specified data catalog.

Endpoints.Name: GetDatabase
Endpoints.Description: Returns a database object for the specified database and data catalog.

Endpoints.Name: GetNamedQuery
Endpoints.Description: Returns information about a single query. Requires that you have access to the workgroup in which the query was saved.

Endpoints.Name: GetNotebookMetadata
Endpoints.Description: Retrieves notebook metadata for the specified notebook ID.

Endpoints.Name: GetPreparedStatement
Endpoints.Description: Retrieves the prepared statement with the specified name from the specified workgroup.

Endpoints.Name: GetQueryExecution
Endpoints.Description: Returns information about a single execution of a query if you have access to the workgroup in which the query ran. Each time a query executes, information about the query execution is saved with a unique ID.

Endpoints.Name: GetQueryResults
Endpoints.Description:
Streams the results of a single query execution specified by QueryExecutionId from the Athena query results location in Amazon S3. For more information, see Working with query results, recent queries, and output files in the Amazon Athena User Guide. This request does not execute the query but returns results. Use StartQueryExecution to run a query.

To stream query results successfully, the IAM principal with permission to call GetQueryResults also must have permissions to the Amazon S3 GetObject action for the Athena query results location.

IAM principals with permission to the Amazon S3 GetObject action for the query results location are able to retrieve query results from Amazon S3 even if permission to the GetQueryResults action is denied. To restrict user or role access, ensure that Amazon S3 permissions to the Athena query location are denied.

Endpoints.Name: GetQueryRuntimeStatistics
Endpoints.Description: Returns query execution runtime statistics related to a single execution of a query if you have access to the workgroup in which the query ran. Query execution runtime statistics are returned only when QueryExecutionStatus$State is in a SUCCEEDED or FAILED state. Stage-level input and output row count and data size statistics are not shown when a query has row-level filters defined in Lake Formation.

Endpoints.Name: GetSession
Endpoints.Description: Gets the full details of a previously created session, including the session status and configuration.

Endpoints.Name: GetSessionStatus
Endpoints.Description: Gets the current status of a session.

Endpoints.Name: GetTableMetadata
Endpoints.Description: Returns table metadata for the specified catalog, database, and table.

Endpoints.Name: GetWorkGroup
Endpoints.Description: Returns information about the workgroup with the specified name.

Endpoints.Name: ImportNotebook
Endpoints.Description: Imports a single ipynb file to a Spark enabled workgroup. The maximum file size that can be imported is 10 megabytes. If an ipynb file with the same name already exists in the workgroup, throws an error.

Endpoints.Name: ListApplicationDPUSizes
Endpoints.Description: Returns the supported DPU sizes for the supported application runtimes (for example, Athena notebook version 1).

Endpoints.Name: ListCalculationExecutions
Endpoints.Description: Lists the calculations that have been submitted to a session in descending order. Newer calculations are listed first; older calculations are listed later.

Endpoints.Name: ListDataCatalogs
Endpoints.Description:
Lists the data catalogs in the current Amazon Web Services account.

In the Athena console, data catalogs are listed as "data sources" on the Data sources page under the Data source name column.

Endpoints.Name: ListDatabases
Endpoints.Description: Lists the databases in the specified data catalog.

Endpoints.Name: ListEngineVersions
Endpoints.Description: Returns a list of engine versions that are available to choose from, including the Auto option.

Endpoints.Name: ListExecutors
Endpoints.Description: Lists, in descending order, the executors that joined a session. Newer executors are listed first; older executors are listed later. The result can be optionally filtered by state.

Endpoints.Name: ListNamedQueries
Endpoints.Description:
Provides a list of available query IDs only for queries saved in the specified workgroup. Requires that you have access to the specified workgroup. If a workgroup is not specified, lists the saved queries for the primary workgroup.

For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

Endpoints.Name: ListNotebookMetadata
Endpoints.Description: Displays the notebook files for the specified workgroup in paginated format.

Endpoints.Name: ListNotebookSessions
Endpoints.Description: Lists, in descending order, the sessions that have been created in a notebook that are in an active state like CREATING, CREATED, IDLE or BUSY. Newer sessions are listed first; older sessions are listed later.

Endpoints.Name: ListPreparedStatements
Endpoints.Description: Lists the prepared statements in the specified workgroup.

Endpoints.Name: ListQueryExecutions
Endpoints.Description:
Provides a list of available query execution IDs for the queries in the specified workgroup. If a workgroup is not specified, returns a list of query execution IDs for the primary workgroup. Requires you to have access to the workgroup in which the queries ran.

For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

Endpoints.Name: ListSessions
Endpoints.Description: Lists the sessions in a workgroup that are in an active state like CREATING, CREATED, IDLE, or BUSY. Newer sessions are listed first; older sessions are listed later.

Endpoints.Name: ListTableMetadata
Endpoints.Description: Lists the metadata for the tables in the specified data catalog database.

Endpoints.Name: ListTagsForResource
Endpoints.Description: Lists the tags associated with an Athena workgroup or data catalog resource.

Endpoints.Name: ListWorkGroups
Endpoints.Description: Lists available workgroups for the account.

Endpoints.Name: StartCalculationExecution
Endpoints.Description: Submits calculations for execution within a session. You can supply the code to run as an inline code block within the request.

Endpoints.Name: StartQueryExecution
Endpoints.Description: Runs the SQL query statements contained in the Query. Requires you to have access to the workgroup in which the query ran. Running queries against an external catalog requires GetDataCatalog permission to the catalog. For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

Endpoints.Name: StartSession
Endpoints.Description: Creates a session for running calculations within a workgroup. The session is ready when it reaches an IDLE state.

Endpoints.Name: StopCalculationExecution
Endpoints.Description:
Requests the cancellation of a calculation. A StopCalculationExecution call on a calculation that is already in a terminal state (for example, STOPPED, FAILED, or COMPLETED) succeeds but has no effect.

Cancelling a calculation is done on a best effort basis. If a calculation cannot be cancelled, you can be charged for its completion. If you are concerned about being charged for a calculation that cannot be cancelled, consider terminating the session in which the calculation is running.

Endpoints.Name: StopQueryExecution
Endpoints.Description:
Stops a query execution. Requires you to have access to the workgroup in which the query ran.

For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

Endpoints.Name: TagResource
Endpoints.Description: Adds one or more tags to an Athena resource. A tag is a label that you assign to a resource. In Athena, a resource can be a workgroup or data catalog. Each tag consists of a key and an optional value, both of which you define. For example, you can use tags to categorize Athena workgroups or data catalogs by purpose, owner, or environment. Use a consistent set of tag keys to make it easier to search and filter workgroups or data catalogs in your account. For best practices, see Tagging Best Practices. Tag keys can be from 1 to 128 UTF-8 Unicode characters, and tag values can be from 0 to 256 UTF-8 Unicode characters. Tags can use letters and numbers representable in UTF-8, and the following characters: + - = . _ : / @. Tag keys and values are case-sensitive. Tag keys must be unique per resource. If you specify more than one tag, separate them by commas.

Endpoints.Name: TerminateSession
Endpoints.Description: Terminates an active session. A TerminateSession call on a session that is already inactive (for example, in a FAILED, TERMINATED or TERMINATING state) succeeds but has no effect. Calculations running in the session when TerminateSession is called are forcefully stopped, but may display as FAILED instead of STOPPED.

Endpoints.Name: UntagResource
Endpoints.Description: Removes one or more tags from a data catalog or workgroup resource.

Endpoints.Name: UpdateDataCatalog
Endpoints.Description: Updates the data catalog that has the specified name.

Endpoints.Name: UpdateNamedQuery
Endpoints.Description: Updates a NamedQuery object. The database or workgroup cannot be updated.

Endpoints.Name: UpdateNotebook
Endpoints.Description: Updates the contents of a Spark notebook.

Endpoints.Name: UpdateNotebookMetadata
Endpoints.Description: Updates the metadata for a notebook.

Endpoints.Name: UpdatePreparedStatement
Endpoints.Description: Updates a prepared statement.

Endpoints.Name: UpdateWorkGroup
Endpoints.Description: Updates the workgroup with the specified name. The workgroup's name cannot be changed. Only ConfigurationUpdates can be specified.

CustomTypes.Name: BatchGetNamedQueryInput
CustomTypes.Description: Contains an array of named query IDs.

CustomTypes.Name: BatchGetQueryExecutionInput
CustomTypes.Description: Contains an array of query execution IDs.

CustomTypes.Name: AclConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: Indicates that an Amazon S3 canned ACL should be set to control ownership of stored query results. When Athena stores query results in Amazon S3, the canned ACL is set with the x-amz-acl request header. For more information about S3 Object Ownership, see Object Ownership settings in the Amazon S3 User Guide.

CustomTypes.Name: ApplicationDPUSizes
CustomTypes.Description: Contains the application runtime IDs and their supported DPU sizes.

CustomTypes.Name: AthenaError
CustomTypes.Description: Provides information about an Athena query error. The AthenaError feature provides standardized error information to help you understand failed queries and take steps after a query failure occurs. AthenaError includes an ErrorCategory field that specifies whether the cause of the failed query is due to system error, user error, or other error.

CustomTypes.Name: NamedQuery
CustomTypes.Description: A query, where QueryString contains the SQL statements that make up the query.

CustomTypes.Name: UnprocessedNamedQueryId
CustomTypes.Description: Information about a named query ID that could not be processed.

CustomTypes.Name: PreparedStatement
CustomTypes.Description: A prepared SQL statement for use with Athena.

CustomTypes.Name: UnprocessedPreparedStatementName
CustomTypes.Description: The name of a prepared statement that could not be returned.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryExecution
CustomTypes.Description: Information about a single instance of a query execution.

CustomTypes.Name: UnprocessedQueryExecutionId
CustomTypes.Description: Describes a query execution that failed to process.

CustomTypes.Name: CalculationConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: Contains configuration information for the calculation.

CustomTypes.Name: CalculationResult
CustomTypes.Description: Contains information about an application-specific calculation result.

CustomTypes.Name: CalculationStatistics
CustomTypes.Description: Contains statistics for a notebook calculation.

CustomTypes.Name: CalculationStatus
CustomTypes.Description: Contains information about the status of a notebook calculation.

CustomTypes.Name: CalculationSummary
CustomTypes.Description: Summary information for a notebook calculation.

CustomTypes.Name: Column
CustomTypes.Description: Contains metadata for a column in a table.

CustomTypes.Name: ColumnInfo
CustomTypes.Description: Information about the columns in a query execution result.

CustomTypes.Name: Tag
CustomTypes.Description: A label that you assign to a resource. In Athena, a resource can be a workgroup or data catalog. Each tag consists of a key and an optional value, both of which you define. For example, you can use tags to categorize Athena workgroups or data catalogs by purpose, owner, or environment. Use a consistent set of tag keys to make it easier to search and filter workgroups or data catalogs in your account. For best practices, see Tagging Best Practices. Tag keys can be from 1 to 128 UTF-8 Unicode characters, and tag values can be from 0 to 256 UTF-8 Unicode characters. Tags can use letters and numbers representable in UTF-8, and the following characters: + - = . _ : / @. Tag keys and values are case-sensitive. Tag keys must be unique per resource. If you specify more than one tag, separate them by commas.

CustomTypes.Name: WorkGroupConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: The configuration of the workgroup, which includes the location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored, the encryption option, if any, used for query and calculation results, whether the Amazon CloudWatch Metrics are enabled for the workgroup and whether workgroup settings override query settings, and the data usage limits for the amount of data scanned per query or per workgroup. The workgroup settings override is specified in EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration (true/false) in the WorkGroupConfiguration. See WorkGroupConfiguration$EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration.

CustomTypes.Name: CustomerContentEncryptionConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: Specifies the KMS key that is used to encrypt the user's data stores in Athena.

CustomTypes.Name: DataCatalog
CustomTypes.Description:
Contains information about a data catalog in an Amazon Web Services account.

In the Athena console, data catalogs are listed as "data sources" on the Data sources page under the Data source name column.

CustomTypes.Name: DataCatalogSummary
CustomTypes.Description: The summary information for the data catalog, which includes its name and type.

CustomTypes.Name: Database
CustomTypes.Description: Contains metadata information for a database in a data catalog.

CustomTypes.Name: Datum
CustomTypes.Description: A piece of data (a field in the table).

CustomTypes.Name: EncryptionConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: If query and calculation results are encrypted in Amazon S3, indicates the encryption option used (for example, SSE_KMS or CSE_KMS) and key information.

CustomTypes.Name: EngineConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: Contains data processing unit (DPU) configuration settings and parameter mappings for a notebook engine.

CustomTypes.Name: EngineVersion
CustomTypes.Description: The Athena engine version for running queries, or the PySpark engine version for running sessions.

CustomTypes.Name: ExecutorsSummary
CustomTypes.Description: Contains summary information about an executor.

CustomTypes.Name: NotebookMetadata
CustomTypes.Description: Contains metadata for notebook, including the notebook name, ID, workgroup, and time created.

CustomTypes.Name: FilterDefinition
CustomTypes.Description: A string for searching notebook names.

CustomTypes.Name: ResultSet
CustomTypes.Description: The metadata and rows that make up a query result set. The metadata describes the column structure and data types. To return a ResultSet object, use GetQueryResults.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryRuntimeStatistics
CustomTypes.Description: The query execution timeline, statistics on input and output rows and bytes, and the different query stages that form the query execution plan.

Fields.Name: Timeline
Fields.Description: Timeline statistics such as query queue time, planning time, execution time, service processing time, and total execution time.

Fields.Name: Rows
Fields.Description: Statistics such as input rows and bytes read by the query, rows and bytes output by the query, and the number of rows written by the query.

CustomTypes.Name: SessionConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: Contains session configuration information.

Fields.Name: EncryptionConfiguration
Fields.Description: If query and calculation results are encrypted in Amazon S3, indicates the encryption option used (for example, SSE_KMS or CSE_KMS) and key information.

CustomTypes.Name: SessionStatus
CustomTypes.Description: Contains information about the status of a session.

CustomTypes.Name: SessionStatistics
CustomTypes.Description: Contains statistics for a session.

CustomTypes.Name: TableMetadata
CustomTypes.Description: Contains metadata for a table.

CustomTypes.Name: WorkGroup
CustomTypes.Description: A workgroup, which contains a name, description, creation time, state, and other configuration, listed under WorkGroup$Configuration. Each workgroup enables you to isolate queries for you or your group of users from other queries in the same account, to configure the query results location and the encryption configuration (known as workgroup settings), to enable sending query metrics to Amazon CloudWatch, and to establish per-query data usage control limits for all queries in a workgroup. The workgroup settings override is specified in EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration (true/false) in the WorkGroupConfiguration. See WorkGroupConfiguration$EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration.

CustomTypes.Name: NotebookSessionSummary
CustomTypes.Description: Contains the notebook session ID and notebook session creation time.

CustomTypes.Name: PreparedStatementSummary
CustomTypes.Description: The name and last modified time of the prepared statement.

CustomTypes.Name: SessionSummary
CustomTypes.Description: Contains summary information about a session.

CustomTypes.Name: WorkGroupSummary
CustomTypes.Description: The summary information for the workgroup, which includes its name, state, description, and the date and time it was created.

CustomTypes.Name: ResultConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: The location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored and the encryption option, if any, used for query and calculation results. These are known as "client-side settings". If workgroup settings override client-side settings, then the query uses the workgroup settings.

CustomTypes.Name: ResultReuseConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: Specifies the query result reuse behavior for the query.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryExecutionContext
CustomTypes.Description: The database and data catalog context in which the query execution occurs.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryExecutionStatus
CustomTypes.Description: The completion date, current state, submission time, and state change reason (if applicable) for the query execution.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryExecutionStatistics
CustomTypes.Description: The amount of data scanned during the query execution and the amount of time that it took to execute, and the type of statement that was run.

CustomTypes.Name: ResultReuseInformation
CustomTypes.Description: Contains information about whether the result of a previous query was reused.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryRuntimeStatisticsTimeline
CustomTypes.Description: Timeline statistics such as query queue time, planning time, execution time, service processing time, and total execution time.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryRuntimeStatisticsRows
CustomTypes.Description: Statistics such as input rows and bytes read by the query, rows and bytes output by the query, and the number of rows written by the query.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryStage
CustomTypes.Description: Stage statistics such as input and output rows and bytes, execution time and stage state. This information also includes substages and the query stage plan.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryStagePlanNode
CustomTypes.Description: Stage plan information such as name, identifier, sub plans, and remote sources.

CustomTypes.Name: ResultConfigurationUpdates
CustomTypes.Description: The information about the updates in the query results, such as output location and encryption configuration for the query results.

CustomTypes.Name: ResultReuseByAgeConfiguration
CustomTypes.Description: Specifies whether previous query results are reused, and if so, their maximum age.

CustomTypes.Name: Row
CustomTypes.Description: The rows that make up a query result table.

CustomTypes.Name: ResultSetMetadata
CustomTypes.Description: The metadata that describes the column structure and data types of a table of query results. To return a ResultSetMetadata object, use GetQueryResults.

CustomTypes.Name: WorkGroupConfigurationUpdates
CustomTypes.Description: The configuration information that will be updated for this workgroup, which includes the location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored, the encryption option, if any, used for query results, whether the Amazon CloudWatch Metrics are enabled for the workgroup, whether the workgroup settings override the client-side settings, and the data usage limit for the amount of bytes scanned per query, if it is specified.

Fields.Name: CustomerContentEncryptionConfiguration
Fields.Description: Specifies the KMS key that is used to encrypt the user's data stores in Athena.

CustomTypes.Name: CalculationExecutionState1
CustomTypes.Description:
The state of the calculation execution. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

CustomTypes.Name: CalculationExecutionState3
CustomTypes.Description:
A filter for a specific calculation execution state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

CustomTypes.Name: CalculationExecutionState4
CustomTypes.Description:
CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

CustomTypes.Name: ColumnNullable2
CustomTypes.Description: Indicates the column's nullable status.

Fields.Name: CustomerContentEncryptionConfiguration
Fields.Description: Specifies the KMS key that is used to encrypt the user's data stores in Athena.

CustomTypes.Name: DataCatalogType1
CustomTypes.Description: The type of data catalog to create: LAMBDA for a federated catalog, HIVE for an external hive metastore, or GLUE for an Glue Data Catalog.

CustomTypes.Name: DataCatalogType2
CustomTypes.Description: The data catalog type.

CustomTypes.Name: DataCatalogType3
CustomTypes.Description: Specifies the type of data catalog to update. Specify LAMBDA for a federated catalog, HIVE for an external hive metastore, or GLUE for an Glue Data Catalog.

CustomTypes.Name: EncryptionOption1
CustomTypes.Description:
Indicates whether Amazon S3 server-side encryption with Amazon S3-managed keys (SSE_S3), server-side encryption with KMS-managed keys (SSE_KMS), or client-side encryption with KMS-managed keys (CSE_KMS) is used.

If a query runs in a workgroup and the workgroup overrides client-side settings, then the workgroup's setting for encryption is used. It specifies whether query results must be encrypted, for all queries that run in this workgroup.

CustomTypes.Name: ExecutorState1
CustomTypes.Description:
A filter for a specific executor state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The executor is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The executor has been started.

REGISTERED - The executor has been registered.

TERMINATING - The executor is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The executor is no longer running.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the executor is no longer running.

CustomTypes.Name: ExecutorState3
CustomTypes.Description:
The processing state of the executor. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The executor is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The executor has been started.

REGISTERED - The executor has been registered.

TERMINATING - The executor is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The executor is no longer running.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the executor is no longer running.

CustomTypes.Name: ExecutorType2
CustomTypes.Description: The type of executor used for the application (COORDINATOR, GATEWAY, or WORKER).

CustomTypes.Name: NotebookType1
CustomTypes.Description: The type of notebook. Currently, the only valid type is IPYNB.

CustomTypes.Name: NotebookType2
CustomTypes.Description: The notebook content type. Currently, the only valid type is IPYNB.

CustomTypes.Name: QueryExecutionState1
CustomTypes.Description:
The state of query execution. QUEUED indicates that the query has been submitted to the service, and Athena will execute the query as soon as resources are available. RUNNING indicates that the query is in execution phase. SUCCEEDED indicates that the query completed without errors. FAILED indicates that the query experienced an error and did not complete processing. CANCELLED indicates that a user input interrupted query execution.

Athena automatically retries your queries in cases of certain transient errors. As a result, you may see the query state transition from RUNNING or FAILED to QUEUED.

Fields.Name: Timeline
Fields.Description: Timeline statistics such as query queue time, planning time, execution time, service processing time, and total execution time.

Fields.Name: Rows
Fields.Description: Statistics such as input rows and bytes read by the query, rows and bytes output by the query, and the number of rows written by the query.

CustomTypes.Name: S3AclOption1
CustomTypes.Description: The Amazon S3 canned ACL that Athena should specify when storing query results. Currently the only supported canned ACL is BUCKET_OWNER_FULL_CONTROL. If a query runs in a workgroup and the workgroup overrides client-side settings, then the Amazon S3 canned ACL specified in the workgroup's settings is used for all queries that run in the workgroup. For more information about Amazon S3 canned ACLs, see Canned ACL in the Amazon S3 User Guide.

Fields.Name: EncryptionConfiguration
Fields.Description: If query and calculation results are encrypted in Amazon S3, indicates the encryption option used (for example, SSE_KMS or CSE_KMS) and key information.

CustomTypes.Name: SessionState1
CustomTypes.Description:
The state of the session. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The session is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The session has been started.

IDLE - The session is able to accept a calculation.

BUSY - The session is processing another task and is unable to accept a calculation.

TERMINATING - The session is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The session and its resources are no longer running.

DEGRADED - The session has no healthy coordinators.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the session and its resources are no longer running.

CustomTypes.Name: SessionState3
CustomTypes.Description:
A filter for a specific session state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The session is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The session has been started.

IDLE - The session is able to accept a calculation.

BUSY - The session is processing another task and is unable to accept a calculation.

TERMINATING - The session is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The session and its resources are no longer running.

DEGRADED - The session has no healthy coordinators.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the session and its resources are no longer running.

CustomTypes.Name: StatementType1
CustomTypes.Description: The type of query statement that was run. DDL indicates DDL query statements. DML indicates DML (Data Manipulation Language) query statements, such as CREATE TABLE AS SELECT. UTILITY indicates query statements other than DDL and DML, such as SHOW CREATE TABLE, or DESCRIBE TABLE.

CustomTypes.Name: WorkGroupState1
CustomTypes.Description: The state of the workgroup.

CustomTypes.Name: WorkGroupState2
CustomTypes.Description: The workgroup state that will be updated for the given workgroup.

CustomTypes.Name: WorkGroupState3
CustomTypes.Description: The state of the workgroup: ENABLED or DISABLED.
